{{Short description="SI derived unit of electric charge}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Coulomb
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Electric charge]], [[Ampere]], [[International System of Units]], [[Elementary charge]]
| unit_system = [[SI derived unit]]
| unit_of = [[Electric charge]]
| symbol = C
| named_after = [[Charles-Augustin de Coulomb]]
| conversion = 1 C = 1 A·s
}}

The '''coulomb''' (symbol: '''C''') is the [[SI derived unit]] of [[electric charge]]. It is named after [[Charles-Augustin de Coulomb]]. One coulomb is defined as the amount of charge transported by a constant [[electric current]] of one [[ampere]] in one [[second]]:

:<math>1\ \text{C} = 1\ \text{A} \cdot 1\ \text{s}</math>

== Relation to elementary charge ==
One coulomb equals approximately 6.241509×10¹⁸ elementary charges:

:<math>1\ \text{C} = \frac{1}{e}\ \text{e} = 6.241509074 \times 10^{18}\ e</math>

where <math>e = 1.602176634 \times 10^{-19}\ \text{C}</math>.

== Coulomb's law ==
{{Main|Coulomb's law}}
The force between two point charges <math>q_1</math> and <math>q_2</math> separated by distance <math>r</math>:

:<math>F = k_e\frac{q_1 q_2}{r^2}</math>
where <math>k_e = 8.9875517923 \times 10^9\ \text{N·m}^2/\text{C}^2</math>

Two charges of 1 C each, 1 m apart, would exert a force of ~9×10⁹ N — approximately the weight of 900,000 tonnes.

== Practical scale ==
* Typical [[lightning]] strike: ~15–350 C
* [[AA battery]] capacity: ~10,000 C
* Charge on a [[capacitor]] in a camera flash: ~0.1 C
* Static charge from walking on carpet: ~10⁻⁶ C

The coulomb is a very large unit — most everyday charge transfers involve microcoulombs (μC) or nanocoulombs (nC).

== Other units ==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Unit !! Value in C
|-
| [[Ampere-hour]] (A·h) || 3,600 C
|-
| [[Faraday constant]] (<math>F</math>) || 96,485 C/mol
|-
| [[Statcoulomb]] (esu) || ~3.336×10⁻¹⁰ C
|}

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Ampere]]
* [[Coulomb's law]]
* [[Electric charge]]
* [[Elementary charge]]
* [[Faraday constant]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:SI derived units]]
[[Category:Units of electric charge]]
[[Category:Electromagnetism]]
